failure_description: ALL 12 RENDERS ARE COMPLETELY BLACK. Max pixel value across entire dataset is 1/255. Mean brightness 0.08/255. This is not a quality issue—this is a total render failure. The output is 100% unusable.

scene1_tbone.worst_problem: COMPLETELY BLACK - render failed or scene has zero lighting
fix_priority: CHECK BLENDER CRASH LOGS - render likely failed
Verify scene has lights (likely missing or turned off)
Check render settings (exposure, color management broken?)
Verify output path not redirected to /dev/null or similar
Check if Cycles render engine is actually being used

scene2_pedestrian.worst_problem: COMPLETELY BLACK - identical failure as scene1
fix_priority: All 12 renders show identical black output
This indicates systematic failure in render pipeline

top_5_problems: CRITICAL: 100% of renders output black frames (max pixel value 1, mean 0.08/255). This is catastrophic failure, not a quality gap.
CRITICAL: All 12 renders identical—indicates systematic pipeline failure, not scene-specific issues.
CRITICAL: No image data visible whatsoever. Either Blender crashed, render engine off, lights deleted, or output corruption.
CRITICAL: Renders are identical file sizes (461-472KB) suggesting they may be placeholder/blank images rather than actual renders.
CRITICAL: No debugging—need to check: Blender logs, scene light setup, render engine settings, output file integrity.
why_not: These are not images. They are black frames with pixel values of 0-1. A lawyer would file a malpractice suit. You cannot demonstrate a collision with completely black video. This is 100% unusable in any courtroom.
regression: SEVERE - Went from 4.75/10 amateur to 0/10 total failure. This is a catastrophic regression.
what_must_change_for_7_plus: 1. FIRST: Verify Blender is actually rendering (check for crashes/errors)
2. Confirm all 4 scenes have proper lighting setup (sun, lamps, emission objects)
3. Verify Cycles render engine is active and not using unsupported output settings
4. Test a single-frame render manually in Blender to confirm output works
5. Check render output path is correct and file is being written
6. If manual test works, rebuild render automation script from scratch
7. Do NOT submit renders to client until you can see images on screen first
8. Once renders have actual image data (brightness > 100/255), then focus on quality metrics
immediate_action_items: Do not bill client for this cycle
Do not submit to client under any circumstances
Open v9_scene1.blend manually in Blender and render frame 1
If you see an image, check the render automation script
If you see black, check scene lighting and render engine
This is show-stopping. Fix before next attempt.
notes: Last audit (v8) scored 4.75/10 with at least visible imagery. V9 has regressed to complete failure. The render pipeline has broken catastrophically. This requires hands-on debugging in Blender, not iterative quality improvements.
